calendar.matters:
HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY AN ORDER FOR SALE OF DWELLING SHOULD NOT ISSUE
Off Calendar May-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Richard Hurlburt Esq., 415-391-6480, via zoom for plaintiff
No other appearances

Not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY AN ORDER FOR SALE OF DWELLING SHOULD NOT ISSUE is GRANTED.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY AN ORDER FOR SALE OF DWELLING SHOULD NOT ISSUE
Off Calendar May-03-2024 Continued To May-24-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Richard Hurlburt; 415-391-6480; appearing as counsel for Judgment Creditor Cullen Laffity.
Carlos Diaz, resident of the subject property and President of Tennesse Street Housing Corporation.
Tina Diaz, resident of subject property.

Mr. and Ms. Diaz appear for the hearing and inform the court that they are residents of the subject property and have been attempting to find legal representation to assist them in filing a Homestead Exemption. The court notes that they have not appeared in this case and are not individually named parties to this action. The parties discuss the standing a corporate entity has to file a Homestead Exemption. The parties agree to continue the hearing to give Mr. and Ms. Diaz an opportunity to find legal counsel. The court rules as follows:

HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY AN ORDER FOR SALE OF DWELLING SHOULD NOT ISSUE is continued to May 24, 2024 at 9:30am in Department 501.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2024 Off Calendar

Affidavit Of Plaintiff Cullen Laffity In Support Of Request For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-05-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar today for a request for Default Judgment against Defendant Tennessee Street Housing Corporation.

Dept:505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Bailiff: Deputy Bastain; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:03:04 a.m. and concluding at 10:37:38 a.m.(D505)

Richard Hurlburt, Esq. representing Plaintiff Cullen Laffity.
Plaintiff Cullen Laffity was placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Tennessee Street Housing Corporation not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint filed December 22, 2022.
Proof of service by mail and acknowledgment of receipt of service, mailed April 18, 2023; Received by April 20, 2023.
Default entered as to Defendant Tennessee Street Housing Corporation on June 21, 2023.
Dismissal as to the Complaint's first cause of action to all defendants on November 30, 2023.
Dismissal filed as to Does 1 - 10 on November 30, 2023.
Notice of hearing for a request for default judgment filed December 11, 2023.

Counsel to summarize the causes of action, provide offer of proof as to what the witness will attest to and to outline the exhibits.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit A: Grant Deed recorded in 1990
Exhibit B: Statement by Common Interest Development Association
Exhibit C: Excerpts of the sections of the By-laws
Exhibit D: Notice of Proposed Membership Termination
Exhibit E: Email dated Dec-06-2021
Exhibit F: Plaintiff's letter
Exhibit G: Email dated Dec-22
Exhibit H: Notice of Membership Termination
Exhibit I: Emails dated Oct-11-2022 and Oct-26-2022 from Plaintiff
Exhibit J: Plaintiff's medical records
Exhibit K: Plaintiff's Paystubs
Exhibit L: Clinical Notes on Plaintiff's condition
Exhibit M: Itemization of medical bills
Exhibit N: Notice of Medi-Cal Lien date Dec-05-2023

Court addresses witness and confirms that statements and declarations made are true and correct.
Court moves the exhibits to evidence.

The Court finds that Plaintiff Cullen Laffity has proven his prima facie case for causes of actions two and three.

Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Cullen Laffity and against Defendant Tennessee Street Housing Corporation as follows:

General Damages
Pain and Suffering $300,00.00
Emotional Distress $300,000.00
Special Damages
Medical Expenses $87,786.00
Loss of Earnings $60,000.00
Other damages $72,000.00
Total $819,786.00

Additional Orders: Plaintiff Cullen Laffity's Membership in the Tennessee Street Housing Corporation is Reinstated will all Attendant Rights and Privileges. (Corporations Code 7341)

Proposed Judgment is signed and executed this day.

Parties stipulate to the return of the exhibits.
